observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended and Management agreed to the preparation and immediate submission of the lacking Bank Reconciliation Statements on all bank accounts including paid/cancelled checks, debit/credit memo and bank statements to make possible the verification on the propriety and accuracy of the Cash-in-Bank balances as appearing in the financial statements during the year. We recommend further that any adjustments/corrections found in the course of reconciliation be effected in the LGU’s books of accounts.

1.12 We also recommended and it was agreed that Management review the MOA and communicate with the source agency on the disposition of the unused funds in its Trust Fund, either to return the funds to the source agency or utilize them in accordance with their intended purpose before closing the dormant account.

3.10 We recommended and Management agreed to de-recognize the cost of the building being demolished. Also, we reiterated our previous recommendation and for which Management also agreed to conduct the physical count of its PPE through the inventory committee and to immediately submit the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the audit team for review and validation. Further, we recommended and the management also agreed initiating a one-time cleansing of PPE accounts under COA Circular 2020-006 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

4.7  We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from incurring or charging expenses that are not related to disaster risk reduction and management activities. Instead, such expenses should be properly charged against the General Fund of the municipality.

5.14 We recommended and it was agreed that the LGU of Medina strictly comply with the provisions of the aforementioned DSWD Memorandum Circular mandating the engagement with the National Food Authority (NFA) for rice procurement.

5.15 Furthermore, we recommended and it was also agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) thoroughly verify the legal documents of suppliers to confirm that their line of business, as stated in their Mayor’s Permit and BIR Certificate of Registration, aligns with the requirements of the procurement project.

6.7  We recommended and it was agreed that the management thru Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) faithfully adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its revised IRR.

6.8  We further recommended and management agreed to discontinue the practice of splitting and/or division or breaking up of contracts into smaller quantities for purposes of evading the necessity of competitive public bidding.

7.8   We recommended and it was agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers together with their complete supporting documents to the audit team as soon as possible.

7.9   Also, it is recommended and it was also accepted to strengthen internal control measure to review and check well the required documents as well as the completeness of signatories before forwarding to the Municipal Mayor for approval.

8.12 We recommended and the management is amenable to discontinue the practice of granting monthly travel allowance to barangay captains without actual travel made to the place of destination. Allow only to the extent that is authorized/allowed by EO No. 77.

8.13 In case of honorarium, we recommended and it was agreed to stop granting the same as it is bereft of legal basis. Otherwise, future grant of such honorarium will be disallowed in audit.

8.14 We further recommended and Management concurred that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer refrain from issuing certificates of appearance without actual travel made as it is highly unlikely that the subject barangay captains would travel to the municipality on a daily basis simply to justify the grant.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the aforementioned lacking/ unsubmitted disbursement vouchers together with their complete 	supporting documents to the audit team on or before February 28, 2024. Else, such transactions will be suspended and/or disallowed in an audit.

Thereafter, submit the financial reports and disbursement vouchers completely based on the monthly check disbursement journal to the audit team to prevent issuance of Notice of Suspensions and/or Disallowances.

Prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for unserviceable properties and instruct the accounting department to facilitate the dropping of the unserviceable items in the agency records.

Demand return of missing PPE items from the concerned accountable personnel and apply administrative sanctions to personnel appropriating government property for personal use.  Conduct a periodic physical count to determine existence and status of recorded property items and prepare reconciliation with LGU records.

The management has already 
created the Inventory/Disposal
Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize the assets/expenses on the year for which these were incurred following the matching principle of accounting. At the end of the year, where payments cannot be made, said obligations shall be recorded as expenses and set the corresponding Accounts Payable based on complete supporting documents.  Further, effect the necessary adjusting entries to record the expenses and Accounts Payable in the books of CY 2019.
observations.managementAction:
The Management complied and
stopped this practice since 2021

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Department to make necessary adjusting entries to record the accrued liability pertaining to earned leave benefits of the employees for the current and previous years after ensuring the correctness of the accumulated leave credit balances as of December 31, 2019. Review at each reporting date and adjust to reflect the current best estimate to ensure that employee’s benefits are properly accounted for and disclosed in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has implemented this observation on staggered 
basis thru appropriating yearly
monetization.

The Management has practiced
this observation in the form of 10%
retention to be released after 1 
year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management review its practice of utilizing the current year’s appropriations to pay prior year’s unrecorded obligations and strictly adhere with existing laws, rules and regulations governing the expenditures within the limits of the current appropriations. The management has already
complied this audit observation
and stop the said practice.

Recommendation:
We recommended management to enforce adherence to the guidelines and procedures enumerated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of the Municipality’s property, plant and equipment account balances to establish verifiability as to existence, condition and accountability.

Management comments:
Management stated that decongesting the motor pool and storage area from unserviceable materials and vehicle equipment was made top priority of the present administration. Yet, it was still in the planning process on how to dispose said properties subject to the COA guidelines. Nevertheless, a committee/team was already created to facilitate the appraisal values and mode of disposal of the unserviceable properties. Further, the local government unit have a standing internal control in-place and have the memorandum receipt (MR) or property acknowledgement receipt (PAR) properly and duly signed by the user. Furthermore, management stated that they will adhere to audit recommendation and assured that proper adjustments and documentation with regards to one time cleansing of the PPE accounts will be made.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze transactions including those pertaining to prior years relative to unidentified discrepancy and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts and furnish COA the corrections made therein.

Management comments:

Nevertheless, the Management committed to comply with this audit observation memorandum.

Recommendation:

Also, send written notices to DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance and other offices where the submission is required that the LGU has no official website to post their monthly reports, thus, posting was made only in the bulletin boards located in three conspicuous public places in the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should strictly implement the full closure and rehabilitation of the existing dumpsite, and the establishment of a sanitary landfill as appropriate final disposal facility. Monitor the implementation of the LGU 10-year solid waste management plan.
observations.managementAction: Management to fully implement its closure plan as approved by the DENR.
